context: Family is one of the first things people ask about when they get to know you - "tienes hermanos?", "tienes hijos?". You don't need the whole family tree; a few words about the people closest to you, plus the verb "tener", cover almost every conversation.
explanation: Talking about family runs on one verb: "tengo" (I have). Put a person after it - "tengo un hermano" - and ask it straight back with "tienes?" (do you have?). "Mi familia" (my family) sets up the whole topic.

examples.es: Tengo un hermano y una hermana.
examples.en: I have a brother and a sister.
Do you have children? - No, I don't.

examples.es: Mi madre se llama Ana y mi padre, Juan.
examples.en: My mother is called Ana and my father, Juan.

phrases.note: Add a person: a brother, a daughter, a partner...
